commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the central behavior changes a trust boundary and multiple open candidate PRs are competing for the same linked bug.; Needs attention: The patch weakens the plugin/provider trust boundary by deriving model-override authorization from plugin-owned config.; Review finding: [P1] Keep summaryModel out of LLM override authorization — src/plugins/config-state.ts:124-128 (sha=a778eeabc2851c3d9a278d91bc922e429ec1e918)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds lossless-claw-specific config normalization that derives an `llm` model-override policy from `config.summaryModel` and adds regression tests for that behavior.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +66. Total +118 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence end-to-end reproduction was run for the original LCM startup failure. The PR defect is source-reproducible from the added normalization that grants LLM override trust from `summaryModel`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the central behavior changes a trust boundary and multiple open candidate PRs are competing for the same linked bug.

target.title: fix: #94289 auto-grant lossless-claw model override from summaryModel config
target.body:
## Summary

Move the lossless-claw `llm` policy auto-population from the runtime LLM resolution path into the plugin config normalization layer (`normalizePluginsConfig`). This ensures `summaryModel`-derived override trust is populated at config load time — matching `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts` but applying to every normal config load.

## Problem

`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` only runs during legacy config migration (`doctor --fix`). For users with a modern plugin-entry config that has `summaryModel` but no `llm` block, the override trust is never granted at startup. LCM compaction fails with "model override denied".

The original v1 fallback in `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` bypassed the documented `plugins.entries.<id>.llm` trust gate and was flagged by ClawSweeper.

## Fix

- **`src/plugins/config-state.ts`** (+56): Added `normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy`, called from `normalizePluginsConfig`. Auto-populates `llm: { allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel] }` when `config.summaryModel` is set but no explicit `llm` block exists. Identical logic to `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts`.

- **`src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.ts`** (-40 +2): Removed runtime `summaryModel` fallback. `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` now reads `normalizePluginsConfig(cfg.plugins).entries[pluginId]?.llm` — the normalized config carries the correct policy.

### Why this approach

1. Config normalization happens once at load time, not per-`complete()` call
2. `llm.allowModelOverride` remains the single trust gate — `summaryModel` populates it rather than bypassing it
3. Consistent with existing `doctor --fix` migration (`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy`)
4. Explicit `llm` block takes priority when configured

## Verification

### Unit tests

```
pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.test.ts  → 21 passed
pnpm test src/plugins/config-state.test.ts                  → 54 passed (50 original + 4 regression)
pnpm test src/context-engine/                               → 18 passed
```

### Real runtime evidence: config normalization behavior

Imported production `dist/` modules directly, tested `normalizePluginsConfig` before vs after:

```
Config: lossless-claw entry, summaryModel="google/gemini-2.5-flash", no llm block

BEFORE (normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver, no fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: undefined
  llm.allowedModels:     []
  → ❌ override would be denied

AFTER (normalizePluginsConfig, with fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: true
  llm.hasAllowedModelsConfig: true
  llm.allowedModels:     ["google/gemini-2.5-flash"]
  → ✅ override granted

Explicit llm block:       ✅ explicit takes priority (unchanged)
No summaryModel:          ✅ no implicit override
No lossless-claw entry:   ✅ no side effects
```

### Real runtime evidence: Gateway startup

Started real OpenClaw Gateway (Node.js v26.2.0, build from `fix/issue-94289-lossless-llm-override`) with lossless-claw plugin installed (`dist/extensions/lossless-claw/`), config with `summaryModel` but no `llm` block:

```
AFTER (with fix):
  http server listening (10 plugins: ..., lossless-claw, ...; 3.6s)
  ✅ Plugin loaded, no "model override denied" error

BEFORE (fix disabled in dist for comparison):
  http server listening (10 plugins: ..., lossless-claw, ...; 3.4s)
  ⚠️ Plugin loads but entries["lossless-claw"].llm → undefined
     → resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy → undefined → "model override denied" at LCM runtime
```

### Full behavior chain

```
normalizePluginsConfig()
  ├─ BEFORE: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() only
  │   └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → undefined
  │       └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → undefined
  │           └─ ❌ "model override denied"
  │
  └─ AFTER: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() + normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy()
      └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → {allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel]}
          └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → policy
              └─ ✅ override granted
```

Fixes: #94289
target.author: lzyyzznl
